slides.heading: Listening (Aizuchi)
slides.content: In Japan, nodding and saying 'un' or 'hai' while others speak shows you are paying attention.

slides.heading: Thinking Sounds
slides.content: Instead of 'um...', say 'Ano...' or 'Eto...'. It makes you sound like a native speaker!

slides.heading: Ending Particles
slides.content: Add 'ne' to the end to say 'right?'. Add 'yo' to show you are giving new info.

slides.heading: Softening Phrases
slides.content: Japanese people rarely say 'No' directly. They might say 'It is a bit...' (Chotto...)

slides.heading: Self-Introduction
slides.content: Start with 'Hajimemashite' and end with 'Yoroshiku onegaishimasu' for a perfect intro.
